action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 22 successfully cached all 45k IN21k ViT features into checkpoints/model.pt for a k-NN classifier. Iteration 23 failed only because a local verification command tried to overwrite dummy_test/test.pt, which is hardlinked to the read-only /data filesystem; the checkpoint itself is intact and predict.py is correct. Analysis: The persistent AdamW linear-head attempts have plateaued around 78-82%, while the proven 83.11% best came from an earlier training run whose checkpoint is lost. The k-NN approach eliminates optimizer/scheduler bugs entirely, uses all training data, and with horizontal-flip TTA should be competitive. Decision: Avoid further local file writes to dodge the read-only hardlink issue and submit the existing k-NN checkpoint for evaluation immediately. If k-NN underperforms, remaining time and iterations are sufficient to retrain a linear head or tune k.
